name: DDGS
what_it_does: Metasearch Python library that aggregates results from multiple search engines (Bing, Brave, DuckDuckGo, Google, Mojeek, Yandex, Yahoo, Wikipedia) into a unified interface. Supports text, image, video, news, and book searches. Includes an MCP server with HTTP and SSE transports, plus a standalone API server.
use_cases: AI agent web search via MCP without per-query API costs
Aggregated multi-engine search for better coverage
News monitoring and research across multiple sources
Image and video search for AI workflows
Book discovery via Anna's Archive integration
not_for: High-volume production search (may hit rate limits from upstream engines)
Guaranteed search result freshness or completeness
best_when: You need free, multi-engine web search for AI agents without paying per-query API fees to commercial search providers.
avoid_when: You need guaranteed uptime, official API access with SLAs, or very high query volumes. Commercial search APIs (Tavily, Brave Search API) may be more reliable.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Uses upstream search engines directly without API keys. Proxy support available for rate limit avoidance.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Completely free. Relies on scraping/unofficial access to search engines, which may break without notice.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on upstream search engine rate limits. Proxy support helps avoid throttling.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: MCP integration is secondary to the Python library. Error handling for rate limits and failed backends not well documented for agent use.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All search operations are read-only and naturally idempotent, though results may vary between calls due to search engine behavior.
known_agent_gotchas: Relies on unofficial/scraping access to search engines - may break without notice
Rate limits are opaque and depend on upstream engines
Results quality and availability varies by backend engine
MCP server requires running the API server separately (Docker or shell script)
Book search uses Anna's Archive which has legal/availability concerns in some jurisdictions
